evidence.text: great benefits with unlimited pto 1000 wellbeing fund flexible remote work busy season usually about 3 months requires working overtime but the rest of the year is pretty standard hours depending on your team client it might be more or less looks really good on a resume and helps with moving to diff companies teams are generally nice to work with and easygoing depends the work generally isn t stressful except during...
great benefits health insurance gym pass health and wellness stipend summers are pretty chill if you don't have an off-year end
good pay unlimited paid time off

great vacation time lots of 'bonus' cash -- official bonus at ye periodic monetary awards throughout the year based on merrit 1500 of 'wellness' money great people easy to excel in teams -- they prioritize initative and being able to do a lot with very little direction keep your ears open and take notes nice offices with subsidized food prices -- lots of renovations nation wide can work from anywhere in us -- still...
unlimited pto great place to start your career people is friendly

the hybrid schedule offers great work-life balance and the unlimited pto provides flexibility when life happens i also really appreciate the focus on well-being things like mental health resources and wellness benefits show that the company cares about its people
work life balance remote work
flexibility and work-life balance are top-tier
great experience friendly culture flexibility work from home
decent work-life balance good pay allows for responsibility and ownership

very strong brand in the private sector not as much in the public sector good medical and fringe benefits with unlimited pto annual 1000 wellbeing fund flexible hybrid work environment offices are nice but we have to pay for parking at our location the firm is very large and ey is siloed if you work in consulting its difficult to work with other business units unless you have leadership connections however there is...
good career growth continuous learning opportunities
ey is one of the big four accounting firms meaning it has a strong reputation in the industry working there can enhance your resume and provide opportunities for career growth
opportunities it provides for career advancement and professional development

excellent training programs and professional development opportunities supportive collaborative team environment and strong mentorship exposure to a wide range of industries and high-profile clients competitive benefits package including generous health and retirement plans global mobility chances to work abroad and on cross-border engagements

okay focusing on the pros of working at ey based on the information we've discussed excellent opportunities for learning and growth ey is frequently cited as a place where employees can continuously learn and develop professionally this includes formal training programs on-the-job experience and exposure to diverse industries and clients strong emphasis on teamwork and collaboration many reviewers highlight a positi...
great teaming and collaboration diverse and inclusive environment open communication culture flexibility to choose areas of interest supportive leadership
supportive friendly work environment that i enjoy
colleagues are friendly and supportive pay is competive
flexible time off friendly environment and teammates
get to work with latest tech great learning opportunities job security
resources to learn if driven
inclusive environment people are fairly nice there
inclusive culture various opportunities for you to try different things

unlimited vacation with no stigma around taking vacation i have been encouraged to take vacation by management fun extracurriculars to get involved in during work time recruiting business development clubs volunteering
if you are not an accountant it feels like you don't matter
salary is not great management overtly lies layoffs not uncommon
health insurance is not good
busy season is not worth the pay literally working forever and not getting paid for it
pay is eh management is not always the greatest
zero loyalty don't care about employees - only care about bottom dollar quality is out the window
people are not as good as they once were
- can't be picky with projects type of work
not learning much technically from the client
pay doesn't match the work flow no work life balance streamline opportunities are low
demanding long hours don't feel like i belong politics unlimited pto is a lie
worst pay team politics favoritism expensive health insurance plan
politics and favoritism to get staffed
- boring repetitive work - toxic culture
you have to learn the reality of bureaucracy and politics
same cons as any other big 4 public accounting firm with the opportunity and experience comes the potential for burn out nothing unique to ey be ready to work 70-80 hour weeks during busy seasons
overworked during busy season can't even take a saturday off for 3-4 months a year very team specific
long hours somewhat chaotic environment not bad though
not sustainable as for long term career development

in cbs roles there are not annual promotions as there are in audit roles and the number of spots for promotion are very limited so it can take a while to promote not much recognition in terms of successes from management transferring to another area can be difficult based on headcount
i can't think of any
when people tell you they hate consulting ey is probably a good example very toxic environment and your managers sms and partners treat you like dirt at the senior level or below
body of work is not always recognized
long-term affects of long hours aren't sustainable
haven't experienced any cons worth mentioning
